title: -Ratification of City Manager Action to Accept a Grant from Americans for the Arts (AFTA) for $20,000 to Support the “Arts Programs” Grants Category

sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution ratifying the action taken by the City Manager to accept a grant from
the Americans for the Arts (AFTA) National Arts Relief fund during recess to receive
$20,000 for the Civic Arts grants program, and to execute any resulting grant
agreements and any amendments.
NEGATIVE EFFECT IF ACTION IS DELAYED UNTIL AFTER COUNCIL RECESS
If action is delayed until after the Council recess, the City will be unable to execute the
grant agreement within the Americans for the Arts deadline. As a result, the City would
forgo $20,000 in National Arts Relief funding, reducing the total funding available for the
Arts Programs Grant category. The funds will be regranted to nonprofit organizations for
arts and cultural programming within the City of Berkeley.
SUMMARY
The City of Berkeley can partially restore previously rescinded National Endowment for
the Arts funding by applying to and accepting funds from the Americans for the Arts
National Arts Relief Fund. Approval of this item would authorize submission of the
application, acceptance of up to $20,000 in grant funding, and execution of the
associated grant agreement to support the Civic Arts Programs Grant category in Fiscal
Year 2026.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
There is no negative fiscal impact to the City’s General Fund. The $20,000 grant from
Americans for the Arts represents one-time, restricted funding that will be regranted
through the existing Civic Arts Grants Program in Fiscal Year 2026. No City match is
required. With this funding the City can provide an additional category of Civic Arts
Programs grants.

E-Mail: manager@berkeleyca.gov Website: https://berkeleyca.gov/

sections.current_situation:
The City of Berkeley was awarded $40,000 from the National Endowment for the Arts
(NEA) for FY26 to support the Civic Arts grant program known as the Arts Programs
grant. Changes in the NEA’s administration led to the agency’s withdrawal of the award
in May of 2025.
In October of 2025, Americans for the Arts offered NEA awardees whose grants were
rescinded the option to apply for the National Arts Relief fund for half of the withdrawn
award. The City of Berkeley can recover $20,000 of the withdrawn NEA award by
applying to and contracting with the National Arts Relief fund. The $20,000, in addition
to a recently awarded California Arts Council grant for $15,900, will be awarded to the
highest-scoring applicants for the FY26 Arts Programs grant. Applications for this
category were collected January through March of 2025, and adjudicated by a grant
review panel in May of 2025.
sections.background:
Funding was requested from the NEA in 2023 to establish a new “Arts Programs Grant”
category in the Civic Arts Grants offerings and better support a wide range of arts and
cultural activities taking place in Berkeley.
The Civic Arts Grants have an annual budget of $658,500 from the City's General Fund
(011), and annually the Civic Arts Grants Program awards funding in three distinct
categories:
1) Individual Artist Projects;
2) Community Festivals, and
3) General Operating Support.
With funding from the NEA along with the local match, the City of Berkeley has
established a fourth Civic Arts Grants funding category – the Arts Programs Grant –
administered in conjunction with the annual Civic Arts Grants process. Arts Program
grants support non-arts nonprofit organizations located in the City of Berkeley that
produce arts or cultural programming in Berkeley led by a Berkeley-based partnering
arts organization or teaching artist, and arts nonprofit organizations located in the
Greater Bay Area (outside of Berkeley) producing arts and cultural programming in the
City of Berkeley.
The Arts Programs grant was anticipated to have a total of $80,000 allocated with a
$40,000 NEA award and a $40,000 required City match. Though the program was
cancelled upon the NEA rescinding the award, applicants can be awarded with the
National Arts Relief funds ($20,000) and California Arts Council fund ($15,900).

Page 3 of 4
Ratification of City Manager Action to Accept a Grant from AFTA for $20,000
to Support the “Arts Programs” Grants Category CONSENT CALENDAR
January 20, 2026
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
There are no environmental sustainability or climate impacts associated with this
recommendation.
sections.rationale:
Accepting funding from the Americans for the Arts’ National Arts Relief Fund would help
the City accomplish one of the five high-level goals articulated in The City of Berkeley’s
Arts and Culture Plan (2018-2027): “Increase Investment in a Vibrant Arts
Community, Strengthen and support Berkeley’s inclusive and culturally rich arts sector
by expanding funding and marketing opportunities for a broader array of arts
organizations.”
sections.alternatives:
The City Council could choose not to accept the grant. If the grant is not accepted,
fewer resources would be available for the Arts Programs Grant category in Fiscal Year
2026, resulting in reduced funding awards to eligible nonprofit arts and cultural
organizations.
CONTACT PERSON
Hilary Amnah, Civic Arts Coordinator, 510-981-7539
sections.attachments:
1: Resolution
Page 3
Page 4 of 4
RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
ADOPT A RESOLUTION RATIFYING THE ACTION TAKEN BY THE CITY MANAGER
DURING RECESS TO ACCEPT A $20,000 GRANT FROM AMERICANS FOR THE
ARTS (AFTA) TO SUPPORT ACCESS TO ARTS AND CULTURAL PROGRAMMING IN
THE CITY OF BERKELEY BY PARTIALLY RESTORING THE CIVIC ARTS GRANTS
PROGRAM FUNDING CATEGORY KNOWN AS THE ARTS PROGRAMS GRANT.
WHEREAS, the Arts and Culture Plan for the City of Berkeley (2017-18) includes the goal
of increasing investment in a vibrant arts community by expanding funding and marketing
opportunities for a broader array of arts organizations; and
WHEREAS, an Arts Program Grant category supports the City’s ability to create
increased access to arts and cultural programming taking place in Berkeley; and
WHEREAS, funding from Americans for the Arts’ National Arts Relief Fund is available
to organizations that had their National Endowment for the Arts grants withdrawn; and
WHEREAS, the City is implementing the second year of the Arts Program Grants in Fiscal
Year 2026 with $20,000 from this grant and $15,900 from a California Arts Council grant.
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that the
Council ratifies the action taken by the City Manager to accept a grant from the Americans
for the Arts to receive to $20,000 for the above purpose; and execute any resultant
agreements and amendments.
